prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a very dark, nearly black frame. Only archive watermarks are visible: a circular “Classic Commercial Archives” mark at lower left and a faint “@ClassicCommercialArchives” text watermark across the lower middle.

It then cuts to a warm-toned close-up of a round, golden-brown bread product held in two hands. The item appears toasted or browned on the surface, with a craggy, uneven texture suggestive of a split English muffin. The background is dark and out of focus, keeping attention on the food and the hands.

The final sampled frame shows two rectangular product packages stacked horizontally in the foreground. The packaging text is partially readable: the upper box says “THOMAS’ SOURDOUGH English muffins,” with “Fork-split” on a yellow panel at the right. The lower box reads “THOMAS’ English MUFFINS,” also with a “Fork-split” label on the right. Behind the boxes is a softly blurred, warm kitchen or dining-room setting, with an indistinct seated person in the background. The focus remains on the branded packages.
